stem: Let \( W \) be a \( [[k]] \)-dimensional subspace of \( \mathbb{R}^{[[n]]} \). What is the dimension of its orthogonal complement \( W^{\perp} \)?
solution: Since \( \dim W + \dim W^{\perp} = [[n]] \), we have \( \dim W^{\perp} = [[n]] - [[k]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This repeats \( \dim W \); the complement has the remaining dimensions.
This is the dimension of the whole space, not the complement.
This adds instead of subtracting from the ambient dimension.
This is off by one from the correct dimension.
This is off by one from the correct dimension.
This is off by two from the correct dimension.
This is off by one from \( \dim W \), not the complement.

stem: Let \( W = \operatorname{span}\{\mathbf{v}_1, \mathbf{v}_2\} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^4 \), where \( \mathbf{v}_1 = (1, [[a]], [[b]], [[c]]) \) and \( \mathbf{v}_2 = ([[p]], [[m2]], [[m3]], [[m4]]) \). What is \( \dim(W^{\perp}) \)?
solution: Row-reducing \( \{\mathbf{v}_1, \mathbf{v}_2\} \) shows \( \dim W = [[rankval]] \), so by \( \dim W + \dim W^{\perp} = 4 \), \( \dim(W^{\perp}) = 4 - [[rankval]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( \dim W \), not the dimension of its orthogonal complement.
This is the dimension of the whole ambient space.
This assumes \( W \) is all of \( \mathbb{R}^4 \).
This overcounts the complement's dimension by one.
This undercounts the complement's dimension by one.
This assumes \( \mathbf{v}_1,\mathbf{v}_2 \) are always independent, ignoring the case \( \mathbf{v}_2 \) is a multiple of \( \mathbf{v}_1 \).
This overcounts the complement's dimension by two.

stem: Let \( A \) be the \( 3\times 4 \) matrix \[ A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & [[s]] & [[t]] & [[m14]] \\ [[p]] & [[m22]] & [[m23]] & [[m24]] \\ [[q]] & [[m32]] & [[m33]] & [[m34]] \end{bmatrix}. \] The left null space of \( A \), \( \{\mathbf{y} \in \mathbb{R}^3 : \mathbf{y}^T A = \mathbf{0}\} \), is the orthogonal complement (within \( \mathbb{R}^3 \)) of the column space of \( A \). What is the dimension of the left null space of \( A \)?
solution: The column space of \( A \) has dimension \( \operatorname{rank}(A) = [[rankval]] \), and it lives in \( \mathbb{R}^3 \). Its orthogonal complement, the left null space, has dimension \( 3 - [[rankval]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the dimension of the COLUMN space, not its orthogonal complement.
This exceeds the ambient dimension of the codomain \( \mathbb{R}^3 \).
This treats the left null space as having negative dimension.
This overcounts the left null space's dimension by one.
This undercounts the left null space's dimension by one.
This uses the number of columns (\(4\)) instead of rows (\(3\)) as the ambient dimension.
This overcounts the left null space's dimension by two.
This exceeds even the number of columns of \( A \).

stem: Let \( W = \operatorname{span}\{\mathbf{u}\} \) be the line through \( \mathbf{u} = ([[u1]], [[u2]], [[u3]]) \) in \( \mathbb{R}^3 \). For what value of \( t \) does the vector \( \mathbf{v} = ([[v1]], [[v2]], t) \) belong to \( W^{\perp} \)?
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign, missing the negative from solving for \( t \).
This forgets to divide by the coefficient of \( t \).
This multiplies by the coefficient of \( t \) instead of dividing by it.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This has the wrong sign.
This is just the third coordinate of \( \mathbf{u} \), unrelated to the orthogonality condition.
